text: Look away from your screen. Find the farthest thing you can see. Stare at it for 20 seconds. Your ciliary muscles just unclenched.
Blink. 10 full, slow blinks right now. Your eyes are drier than you think.
One deep breath. In through your nose for 4 seconds. Out through your mouth for 6. That's it.
Unclench your jaw. You were clenching it. Everyone does while coding.
Drop your shoulders. They were up near your ears again.
Check your screen brightness. If it's the brightest thing in your field of vision, turn it down or add a lamp.
Dehydration reduces cognitive performance by 12% before you even feel thirsty. Drink a glass of water.
Your neck is tilted forward right now. Every inch adds 10 lbs of force on your spine. Tuck your chin, push the back of your head toward the ceiling.
Coffee has a 5-6 hour half-life. If it's after 2pm, that cup will be at 50% strength at 8pm. Water might be the better call.
You're probably death-gripping your mouse. Loosen up. Rest your hand on it instead of clutching it.
Your diaphragm compresses when you slouch. Less oxygen per breath, less oxygen to your brain. Sit up tall for the next 10 minutes.
Sip water. Your code quality correlates with hydration more than caffeine. Not a metaphor, your prefrontal cortex runs on glucose dissolved in water.
Your forearm muscles tense up during long sessions. Extend your arm, palm up, gently pull fingers back for 15 seconds. Switch hands. Wrist flexor stretch.
Roll your shoulders back 5 times, then forward 5 times. That tension was compressing your neck nerves.
Your hip flexors shorten when you sit. Stand up, step one foot back, sink into a lunge. 20 seconds each side.
Wrist extension stretch: extend arm, palm down, press back of hand toward you. 15 seconds each side.
Quick desk exercise: palms flat on desk, fingers forward, lean weight onto them gently. You'll feel it through your forearms. 15 seconds.
Your thoracic spine rounds forward when you code. Stand in a doorway, forearms on frame, lean through. 20 seconds. Opens your chest.
20 seconds of big arm circles. Your body temp drops during long sitting and muscles stiffen. Get blood flowing.
Debugging frustration triggers shallow breathing. 3 belly breaths: inhale 4s through nose, exhale 6s through mouth. Nervous system reset.
You've been sitting for 90+ minutes. Blood flow to your legs drops nearly 50%. Stand up. 10 calf raises. Walk to the window. 60 seconds minimum.
Real break time. Not phone-scrolling. Stand up, walk to a window, look outside for 60 seconds. Eyes, brain, and body all reset at once.
Your glutes turned off an hour ago. Stand up, squeeze them hard for 10 seconds, release. 3 times. Then walk around for a minute.
Mental fatigue isn't just being tired. Your prefrontal cortex depleted its glucose. A 5-minute walk outside resets executive function better than caffeine.
